subject: docs(db): reflect trigger lockdown + backfilled ledger; record two AI mistakes
body:
The DoS window on spatial_ref_sys is closed by the trigger shipped in
19ed26d; the canonical RLS fix is still pending Supabase support but
no longer blocking. Documentation sync:

- CLAUDE.md project-status line: 18/18 applied + trigger-based
  lockdown live; RLS still pending.
- CLAUDE.md "Known production risks": severity downgraded but entry
  retained (lint still flags, and trigger isn't as robust as RLS
  across platform upgrades). Documents the debug artifacts
  (_trigger_probe + _trigger_probe_fn) on the remote that we can't
  drop until support does.
- CLAUDE.md AI Notes: new rule — before declaring a Supabase DDL
  "blocked", enumerate Postgres's privilege matrix. Different
  statements check different privileges. Had I done this on first
  block (ALTER TABLE → ownership), I'd have found CREATE TRIGGER is
  permitted and not needed the user's second prompt.
- docs/USER_ACTIONS.md: rewritten support-ticket template. Priority
  drops from HIGH to MEDIUM because exploitation is blocked. Ticket
  now covers three operations server-side: (1) ENABLE RLS + policy,
  (2) DROP the bridge trigger + function (redundant once RLS lands),
  (3) DROP the investigation artifacts (_trigger_probe stuff).
  Post-resolution cleanup path: delete migration 20260421000001 from
  the repo, update lockdown test to assert on the RLS error code.
- docs/AI_MISTAKES.md: new entry — declared a Supabase fix blocked
  without exhausting Postgres's privilege matrix. Enumerates the
  per-statement privilege check so future sessions don't repeat.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: rewritten with final state — 130/130 tests,
  trigger live, support ticket as outstanding.

Also backfilled the `statements` column on the 13 schema_migrations
rows inserted earlier this session (plus the new 20260421000001 row).
This is remote-side only, no repo change, but worth noting: matches
what `supabase db push` would have populated. Reversed from the
earlier "cosmetic; skipped" position because the user's directive
was explicit about no skipping.

Tags: documentation, migrations, supabase, security
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: db-sync
Semver: patch
